seoTitle: How to Retrieve Deleted or Voided Transactions in QBO
metaDescription: Use the QuickBooks Online Audit Log to find deleted or voided transactions and rebuild them manually with the original details.
intro: Accidentally deleting an invoice or voiding a check doesn't have to mean the record is gone for good. QuickBooks Online logs every change through its Audit Log, which you can use to trace exactly what was removed and rebuild it. This guide shows how to filter that log and reconstruct a missing transaction from its captured history.

steps.heading: Open the Audit Log
steps.body: Click the Gear icon, go to Tools, and select Audit Log to open the centralized change history workspace.

steps.heading: Filter the activity by date and event
steps.body: Use the filter panel to set a date range, choose specific users, and check the Transactions box under events to narrow the results.

steps.heading: Find deleted or voided rows
steps.body: Scan the Action column for entries explicitly marked as Deleted or Voided to locate the transaction you're trying to recover.

steps.heading: View the historical payload
steps.body: Click View on the flagged row, then expand the history to see the exact original amount, category, and vendor or customer details.

steps.heading: Rebuild the entry manually
steps.body: Keep the audit details open in one tab, open a new transaction form, and re-enter the saved values to restore the record.

faq.question: Can I restore a deleted transaction with one click?
faq.answer: No, QuickBooks Online doesn't support a one-click undo for deleted transactions, so you need to manually rebuild it using the audit log details.

faq.question: Who can access the Audit Log?
faq.answer: Access is typically limited to admin users, so you may need elevated permissions to view it.

faq.question: How far back does the Audit Log go?
faq.answer: The log generally tracks the full history of your company file, though very old entries may take longer to filter through.

faq.question: Will rebuilding the transaction affect my reconciliation?
faq.answer: It can if the original entry was already reconciled, so double-check your bank register after rebuilding to confirm balances still line up.
